<current_datetime>2026-04-03T01:05:23.278Z</current_datetime>

Walk me through how I would set up crash dump collection for a
NativeAOT .NET 11 app in Kubernetes, assuming createdump is available
at /opt/tools/createdump. What pod spec changes are needed?

input.initialPrompt:
Walk me through how I would set up crash dump collection for a
NativeAOT .NET 11 app in Kubernetes, assuming createdump is available
at /opt/tools/createdump. What pod spec changes are needed?

data.content:
Here's a concise walkthrough:

## 1. Environment Variables in the Container Spec

```yaml
env:
  # Tell the .NET runtime where createdump lives
  - name: DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName
    value: /tmp/dumps/core.%p.%t
  - name: DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump
    value: "1"
  - name: DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType
    value: "4"            # Full heap dump (Triage=1, Mini=2, Heap=3, Full=4)
  - name: DOTNET_CreateDumpPath
    value: /opt/tools/createdump
```

> **NativeAOT note (.NET 11):** NativeAOT apps respect these `DOTNET_` env vars for crash dump generation starting in .NET 8+. Ensure your app is published with the crash-dump support not trimmed (the default).

## 2. Volume for Dump Storage

```yaml
volumes:
  - name: dump-volume
    emptyDir:
      sizeLimit: 4Gi        # size to your app's memory footprint
```

And in the container:

```yaml
volumeMounts:
  - name: dump-volume
    mountPath: /tmp/dumps
```

## 3. Security Context — `ptrace` Capability

`createdump` needs `SYS_PTRACE` to attach to the crashing process:

```yaml
securityContext:
  capabilities:
    add: ["SYS_PTRACE"]
```

## 4. (Optional) Sidecar or Init Container to Retrieve Dumps

A simple sidecar that watches for dumps and uploads them:

```yaml
- name: dump-uploader
  image: your-registry/dump-uploader:latest
  volumeMounts:
    - name: dump-volume
      mountPath: /tmp/dumps
      readOnly: true
  command: ["sh", "-c", "inotifywait -m /tmp/dumps -e create | while read f; do upload-to-blob \"$f\"; done"]
```

## 5. Full Pod Spec Skeleton

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: my-nativeaot-app
spec:
  volumes:
    - name: dump-volume
      emptyDir: { sizeLimit: 4Gi }
  containers:
    - name: app
      image: your-registry/my-nativeaot-app:latest
      securityContext:
        capabilities:
          add: ["SYS_PTRACE"]
      env:
        - name: DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump
          value: "1"
        - name: DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType
          value: "4"
        - name: DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName
          value: /tmp/dumps/core.%p.%t
        - name: DOTNET_CreateDumpPath
          value: /opt/tools/createdump
      volumeMounts:
        - name: dump-volume
          mountPath: /tmp/dumps
```

## Key Gotchas

| Issue | Fix |
|---|---|
| Dumps silently fail | Verify `createdump` is executable and `SYS_PTRACE` is granted |
| Pod evicted after crash | `sizeLimit` on `emptyDir` — keep it proportional to app RSS |
| `createdump` not found | Confirm the binary exists at the exact `DOTNET_CreateDumpPath`; NativeAOT doesn't bundle it automatically |
| PodSecurityPolicy / PSA blocks `SYS_PTRACE` | Add an exception in your namespace's `PodSecurityAdmission` config or PSP |

This setup will cause the .NET runtime to invoke `/opt/tools/createdump` on any unhandled crash, writing a full dump to the shared volume.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on setting up crash dump collection for a NativeAOT .NET 11 app in Kubernetes. This is a knowledge/advisory question, not a code change task. I'll provide a concise, practical walkthrough.
